verdict_summary: OK_with_anomaly — APF warning ('차단') IS rendered as expected, BUT delivery point shifted from S2 (chat POST) to navigation-time GET https://grok.com/. PII prompt was not actually submitted because the chat UI is unreachable from Chrome (every grok.com navigation, even fresh incognito tab, returns the apf-warning HTML page). Strictly: expected_text '차단' present → warning system functional. Anomaly: blocking point appears earlier than the historical S2 baseline (2026-05-07).

evidence.warning_text_quote: 보안 경고 / 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다. (This request was blocked due to sensitive information.)
evidence.answer_area_state: no chat UI rendered — entire response body is APF-injected warning HTML <div id="apf-warning-banner">; SPA never loaded; document title set to '보안 정책 - 접근 차단'
evidence.console_errors_excerpt: Not captured — DevTools Console was opened on a Google search misnavigation tab, then closed. After correct navigation to grok.com, only the warning HTML loaded so no app JS executed → no app-side console errors expected. Browser-level: no error overlays observed in the rendered page.
evidence.url_after_submit: https://grok.com/  (no submit performed; PII prompt never sent because chat UI was never reachable)

recommendation: Investigate why APF blocks Chrome's GET https://grok.com/ at navigation time. Three discrimination tests run on test PC: (1) Chrome regular tab → blocked, (2) Chrome incognito (no SW/cookies/cache) → blocked, (3) PowerShell Invoke-WebRequest with Chrome UA + matching Accept headers → returns real grok HTML (200, 400KB). This indicates APF is discriminating on something Chrome-specific (TLS JA3 fingerprint, HTTP/2 frame ordering, ALPN, or some Chrome-only header) rather than URL/PII content. Either: (a) APF added a UA/fingerprint-based hostname block for grok.com (intentional? then update test definition), or (b) APF has a regex false-positive on Chrome's SNI/cookie bytes (unintentional → bug). DO NOT promote to 'PASS_S2' — actual S2 (POST-time PII detection on chat submission) was NOT exercised.
notes: Phase B canary #8 (4-day staleness check). KEY FINDINGS: (a) warning text '차단' IS present so naive expected_text match passes; (b) but blocking happens at GET / navigation, not at chat POST, so the test does not actually verify S2 PII detection on grok chat; (c) view-source:https://grok.com confirmed APF-injected HTML with id='apf-warning-banner' style='position:fixed...background:#fef3f2;border:2px solid #d92d20;color:#7a271a' — standard apf-warning markup; (d) PowerShell Invoke-WebRequest to same URL returns genuine grok SPA HTML, so APF blocks Chrome but not other clients — Chrome-fingerprint-based discrimination. L-068 watch: no AUTH_REQUIRED text seen; the page is purely the APF warning, no Cloudflare/grok login redirect. Recommend dev PC review whether grok was intentionally hostname-blocked in latest APF rule push (which would explain the Chrome-specific behavior if APF identifies browsers via JA3 and applies a stricter rule), or if this is unintended drift.
